subject: ARTHIST
course_description: Selected topics in history for students in non-history originating (cross-listed) courses. May be repeated for credit when topic varies.
course_notes: (Same as REL 270 3, ANT 285 4, SOC 289 1) (This course satisfies the Religion major requirement:- Tradition-Christianity, Theme.) (Complete Topic: Apocalypse and Rebellion: Millennial Movements in Latin America) (The relationship between religious radicalism and social-political dissent has long stood as one of the most compelling dimensions of Latin American history. This course traces the recurring emergence of millenarian and messianic movements from the colonial period through the national era, examining how visions of an imminent end shaped collective action, resistance, and hope. We will study the formation, diffusion, and eventual suppression of these movements within the wider context of major social, economic, and political transformations in Ibero-America. Central to our work will be an analysis of millennial rhetoric and, more broadly, the dynamics of authority, persuasion, and the social construction of reality that sustained these movements. Throughout the course, we will also confront the ways race, class, and gender inequalities informed millennial projects, apocalyptic expectations, and utopian quests for redemption.)
